techniques.evidence_quote: The attacker used a long-term access key to gain initial access.
the attacker first issued a ListUsers API call using the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user2 IAM user from the AWS command line interface (aws-cli)
the attacker issued two calls to the UpdateLoginProfile API; one for the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user1 IAM user (succeeded)
create two new IAM users with accompanying long-term access keys
the attacker issued an AttachUserPolicy API call to attach an AdministratorAccess policy to the newly created IAM user — allowing the attacker to elevate their privileges in AWS.
our working theory that the AWS access keys for the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user2 account were discovered by the attacker in a publicly available code repository.
this action was taken in preparation for starting multiple large EC2 instances for cryptocurrency mining

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained long-term AWS access keys for IAM user comp_user2 from a publicly available code repository.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: our working theory that the AWS access keys for the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user2 account were discovered by the attacker in a publicly available code repository.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used compromised long-term access keys for comp_user2 to authenticate to AWS via aws-cli.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker used a long-term access key to gain initial access.

evidence_chunks.quote: The attacker used a long-term access key to gain initial access.
The attacker used a long-term access key to gain initial access.

attack_chain.description: Attacker ran ListUsers API call from aws-cli to enumerate IAM users in the account, discovering comp_user1.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker first issued a ListUsers API call using the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user2 IAM user from the AWS command line interface (aws-cli)

attack_chain.description: Attacker called UpdateLoginProfile to set a console password for comp_user1 (succeeded) and comp_user2 (failed with NoSuchEntityException).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker issued two calls to the UpdateLoginProfile API; one for the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user1 IAM user (succeeded)

attack_chain.description: Attacker logged into the AWS Management Console as comp_user1 from Indonesia without MFA.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: CloudTrail logs recorded a ConsoleLogin from the arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/comp_user1 account.

attack_chain.description: Attacker called CreateUser to create two new IAM users (created_user1 and created_user2) as a persistence foothold.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: create two new IAM users with accompanying long-term access keys

evidence_chunks.quote: create two new IAM users with accompanying long-term access keys
create two new IAM users with accompanying long-term access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker issued CreateAccessKey and CreateLoginProfile for the newly created users to establish both programmatic and console access.
Attacker called AttachUserPolicy to attach the AdministratorAccess managed policy to one of the newly created users, escalating privileges.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker issued an AttachUserPolicy API call to attach an AdministratorAccess policy to the newly created IAM user — allowing the attacker to elevate their privileges in AWS.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used a newly created IAM user to call RequestServiceQuotaIncrease to raise the EC2 quota, in apparent preparation for spinning up large EC2 instances for cryptocurrency mining. Activity was stopped before mining occurred.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker used one of the newly created IAM users to make a call to the RequestServiceQuotaIncrease API in order to increase the EC2 quota. It's our opinion that this action was taken in preparation for starting multiple large EC2 instances for cryptocurrency mining.

initial_access_vector: Long-term AWS IAM access keys (AKIA) for user comp_user2 exposed in a publicly available code repository, used by attacker via aws-cli from an Indonesian IP.
persistence_mechanism: Attacker created two new IAM users with long-term access keys and console login profiles, and changed the console password of existing IAM user comp_user1 via UpdateLoginProfile.
privilege_escalation: AttachUserPolicy used to attach the AWS managed AdministratorAccess policy to a newly created IAM user.
